exercises.title: Design Your Morning Routine
exercises.description: Create a morning routine with 5 elements: hydration, movement, mindfulness, priority review, and a transition activity. Use the Two-Minute Rule for each element (start small). Write out the specific times and what you'll do. Practice for 2 weeks and track completion.
hints: Start with what you already do (current routine) and add one element at a time
Use the Two-Minute Rule: if 10 min meditation is too much, start with 3 deep breaths
Place items physically where you'll see them (water glass by bed, shoes by door)

solution.code:
MORNING ROUTINE TEMPLATE:

6:30 AM - Wake up (consistent time, no snooze)
6:35 AM - Drink 16 oz water (glass on nightstand)
6:40 AM - 5 min stretching (yoga mat by bed)
6:45 AM - 3 deep breaths + set today's intention
6:50 AM - Shower + get ready
7:20 AM - Breakfast (protein: eggs or yogurt)
7:35 AM - Review 3 priorities (Notion board)
7:45 AM - Transition (commute or walk around block)
8:00 AM - Start deep work

TWO-MINUTE VARIANTS (for busy mornings):
• Hydration: Drink water while waiting for coffee
• Movement: 2 min stretching at desk
• Mindfulness: 3 deep breaths in the shower
• Priorities: Glance at Notion on phone
• Transition: Walk to desk instead of rolling chair

TRACKING (2 weeks):
| Day | Hydrate | Move | Mind | Priorities | Transition |
|-----|---------|------|------|------------|------------|
| Mon | ✓       | ✓    | ✓    | ✓          | ✓          |
| Tue | ✓       | ✓    | ✗    | ✓          | ✓          |
| Wed | ✓       | ✓    | ✓    | ✓          | ✗          |
...
Completion rate: 87% (target: 80%+)
Verdict: Routine is working. Add meditation next week.

exercises.title: Build a Habit Stack
exercises.description: Identify 3 existing habits you do automatically every day. Attach a new goal-related behavior to each using the 'After I [existing habit], I will [new habit]' formula. Practice all 3 for 2 weeks and track which ones stick.
hints: Choose habits that happen at consistent times (coffee, lunch, closing laptop)
Make the new habits very small (2-5 minutes) to start
Write the stack down and place it where you'll see it

solution.code:
HABIT STACK DESIGN:

STACK 1: Morning Priority
After I pour my coffee, I will review today's 3 priorities on Notion.
Time: 2 minutes
Trigger: Coffee smell (strong sensory cue)
Reward: Feeling prepared and in control

STACK 2: Learning
After I finish lunch, I will read 10 pages of a tech book.
Time: 15 minutes
Trigger: Closing my lunch container
Reward: Knowledge accumulation, book progress tracker

STACK 3: Shutdown
After I close my laptop at 5 PM, I will write tomorrow's 3 priorities.
Time: 3 minutes
Trigger: Laptop closing (physical signal)
Reward: Mental clarity, work-life boundary

TRACKING (2 weeks):
| Stack | Week 1 | Week 2 | Rate |
|-------|--------|--------|------|
| Coffee priorities | 6/7 | 7/7 | 93% |
| Lunch reading | 5/7 | 6/7 | 79% |
| Shutdown priorities | 4/7 | 5/7 | 64% |

ANALYSIS:
• Stack 1 (morning) sticks easily — strong sensory trigger
• Stack 2 (lunch) works well — consistent daily event
• Stack 3 (shutdown) needs work — add physical signal (close laptop lid audibly)

exercises.title: Design a Shutdown Ritual
exercises.description: Create a 10-minute end-of-day shutdown ritual that helps you mentally disconnect from work. Include: task review, email scan, tomorrow's plan, physical signal, and transition activity. Practice for 1 week and note how your evening quality changes.
hints: Make the physical signal very clear (saying 'shutdown complete' out loud sounds silly but works)
The ritual should be consistent — same time, same steps, every day
The transition activity is crucial: it creates a mental boundary between work and rest

solution.code:
SHUTDOWN RITUAL TEMPLATE:

4:50 PM - Stop working on current task
4:55 PM - Quick email/Slack scan (5 min max)
5:00 PM - Review today's task list
         - Check off completed items ✓
         - Move incomplete items to tomorrow
5:03 PM - Write tomorrow's 3 priorities
         - Schedule them on calendar
5:06 PM - Physical signal: close all work tabs
         - Say out loud: 'Shutdown complete'
5:07 PM - Transition activity:
         - Option A: Walk around the block (10 min)
         - Option B: Change into casual clothes
         - Option C: Cook dinner (hands-on activity)
5:10 PM - Work is done. No checking email until tomorrow.

WHAT TO AVOID:
• Checking email 'one last time' after shutdown
• Keeping work Slack open on your phone
• Thinking about tomorrow's tasks after shutdown
• Working past your shutdown time 'just to finish one thing'

WEEK 1 RESULTS:
• Completed ritual: 5/7 days (71%)
• Evening anxiety: Reduced noticeably
• Sleep quality: Improved (less rumination)
• Weekday vs weekend: Started feeling more distinct
